items.title: Johor allocates RM110,000 to upgrade teachers' rooms in Tanjung Surat schools
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The Johor government has continued its support for educators with a RM110,000 allocation for teachers' rooms under the Tanjung Surat state constituency, in a move aimed at improving workplace comfort and teaching conditions. Johor Education and Information Committee chairman Aznan Tamin said the assistance went beyond financial aid, describing it as recognition of educators' sacrifices in shaping future generations. He added that improving teachers' rooms would directly enhance the working environment, which in turn supports better teaching quality in schools.

items.title: Tioman tourism hit; BN rep asks state govt to waive ferry fuel surcharge
items.summary: ROMPIN: A Barisan Nasional lawmaker has voiced concern about slowing visitor numbers to Pulau Tioman since the ferry fuel surcharge came into effect on April 20. "Exemptions from the ferry fuel surcharge to the island would help boost visitor arrivals and encourage more people to travel domestically," he said when contacted today. "Then, the Transport Ministry introduced a targeted nationwide fuel surcharge mechanism.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends at intraday high on improved local sentiment
items.summary: Among heavyweights, Maybank rose 12 sen to RM11.22, Public Bank added six sen to RM4.78, CIMB improved 13 sen to RM7.78, IHH Healthcare gained four sen to RM8.81, while Tenaga Nasional was six sen lower at RM14.52. On the most active list, Luster Industries eased by half-a-sen to 3.5 sen, Zetrix AI fell one sen to 86 sen, VS Industry dropped 1.5 sen to 21 sen, while UEM Sunrise rose 7.5 sen to 66.5 sen, and MRCB bagged two sen to 33.5 sen. Top gainers included Nestle, which garnered RM3.18 to RM102.30, Fraser & Neave advanced 72 sen to RM31.40, KESM surged 46 sen to RM4.19, United Plantations climbed 38 sen to RM32.00, and Dutch Lady perked up 28 sen to RM32.78. As for top losers, Malaysian Pacific Industries, which shaved off 38 sen to RM37.50, Allianz decreased 36 sen to RM20.80, Kelington and Batu Kawan slipped 16 sen each to RM6.22 and RM20.98, respectively, and UWC lost 15 sen to RM5.12.

items.title: Counterfeit medical gear syndicate smashed; goods worth RM1.34mil seized in raid
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry crippled a suspected counterfeit health equipment syndicate following a raid on a warehouse and packing centre here, seizing goods worth nearly RM1.34 million. In a joint operation in last month, enforcement officers from Putrajaya and Johor, moved in on a premises believed to be a storage and distribution hub for online sales of health screening devices bearing unauthorised trademarks. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said inspections uncovered a wide range of medical devices suspected to be counterfeit and intended for distribution to consumers.

items.title: Germany approves new rescue bid for stranded whale
items.summary: INSEL POEL: German officials today gave the green light for a fresh rescue attempt for a humpback whale which has been stranded off the country's Baltic Sea coast for over a month. The 13-metre (over 40 foot) whale and its struggle for survival have gripped Germany since the sea mammal beached on a sandbank near the city of Luebeck, far from its natural habitat. After several initial attempts failed, two entrepreneurs came forward to finance a new rescue bid which will involve loading the cetacean onto a special barge and carrying it out to deeper waters.

items.title: Policy reforms needed to tackle Sabah landfill, marine pollution issues
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Comprehensive impact studies and policy reforms are crucial to addressing critical waste management issues in the Sabah state capital. Datuk Jasnih Daya (Gagasan-Pantai Dalit) said the 46.5-hectare Kayu Madang landfill, the primary disposal site for the west coast, including Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran, Penampang, Putatan, and Kota Belud, receives about 800 tonnes of solid waste daily. He said the facility, which replaced the Lokawi landfill in 1996, is nearing its maximum capacity and entering its final operational phase, placing immense strain on the state waste management system.

items.title: Construction company owner held over RM90,000 drone purchase fraud
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has arrested a construction company owner for submitting falsified documents to a financial institution for the purchase of a drone worth RM90,000. A MACC source said the man, in his 50s, was detained at noon yesterday after giving his statement at the Kedah MACC office. Kedah MACC director Datuk Nazli Rasyid Sulong confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Two company directors jailed, fined RM350,000 over illegal e-waste handling
items.summary: SEGAMAT: Two company directors were jailed and fined a total of RM350,000 yesterday after pleading guilty to multiple offences linked to the illegal handling of electronic waste at an industrial site here. Johor Department of Environment (DoE) director Mohd Famey Yusoff said the convictions marked a significant enforcement action against unlawful recycling activities involving scheduled waste coded SW110 (electrical and electronic waste). He was sentenced to one day's imprisonment and fined RM100,000 for constructing a recovery plant without Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) approval under Section 34A(6), and a further one day's jail with a RM100,000 fine for placing scheduled waste without approval under Section 34B(1)(a).

items.title: Tabung Haji urges Malaysian Haj pilgrims to focus on worship, limit social media use
items.summary: MECCA, April 28 — Amid the excitement of sharing moments in the Holy Land, Haj pilgrims are reminded not to let social media distract them from the primary purpose of their pilgrimage. Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) Haj Guidance Operations director Shahrin Awaluddin said the trend, particularly among younger pilgrims, could affect the devotion and completeness of their haj rituals. Meanwhile, Shahrin assured that TH continuously monitors the overall welfare of Malaysian pilgrims throughout their stay in the Holy Land.

items.title: Melor rep probe: Kelantan police to submit IP to DPP after statement over viral video mocking officers
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 28 — Police will submit the investigation paper (IP) involving Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud to the Deputy Public Prosecutor in the near future after his statement was recorded yesterday. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said Wan Rohimi gave good cooperation throughout the statement-recording process conducted at the Kota Bharu district police headquarters (IPD). “Wan Rohimi attended the Kota Bharu IPD yesterday to give his statement regarding the issue linked to him.

items.title: Melaka raids dismantle scam ‘calling centres’ offering sex, love and investment fraud schemes; 45 arrested
items.summary: MELAKA, April 28 — Police have dismantled two “calling centre” syndicates involved in scams offering non-existent sex services, as well as love scams and fake investment schemes, following raids on two premises in Taman Kota Bendahari here. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said that 45 foreign nationals from China, Vietnam and Indonesia, aged between 20 and 44, were arrested in the operations conducted at 11am on April 23. “In the first raid, police arrested 19 Chinese men, one Chinese woman and one Indonesian woman aged between 28 and 44.

items.title: Century-old Waterfall Cafe razed after failed relocation talks
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government said all negotiation efforts with Waterfall Cafe had failed, with the operator rejecting multiple relocation proposals despite repeated notices to vacate the premises. This came as enforcement action today saw the demolition of the 103-year-old structure at Taman Botani here. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state had engaged continuously with affected stakeholders, including the cafe operator, in an effort to reach a fair and practical resolution.

items.title: Establish Sabah east coast energy reserve, Silam assemblyman tells state govt
items.summary: Datuk Dr Mohammad Yusof Apdal (Warisan-Silam) said Sabah and Sarawak are major contributors to the nation's oil revenue, yet storage capacity for emergency use remains limited. "Establishing a strategic storage zone on Sabah's east coast would reduce the region's exposure to market price fluctuations or global supply disruptions. 'First, it would help narrow the economic gap between Sabah's west and east coasts.

items.title: RM2bil sovereign AI cloud to safeguard security, boost talent - PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's RM2 billion investment in a sovereign AI cloud is aimed at strengthening national security, developing local talent, and ensuring the country does not remain only a consumer of global technology, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today. Speaking at the Temu Anwar session at UCSI University today, Anwar said that while Malaysia maintains an open investment framework and continues to welcome global technology investments, it also has safeguards for national security and data privacy He was responding to a question from A-Levels student Thashenndhra Jeyraj, who asked whether the sovereign cloud initiative would meaningfully benefit local startups and small and medium enterprises, or primarily reinforce the dominance of large global technology companies. Anwar said the policy reflects Malaysia's approach of maintaining an open digital economy while introducing targeted safeguards for national security and data privacy.

items.title: How should Ukrainian teens committing acts of betrayal be judged?
items.summary: Just two days before, Russia had struck a hospital. Fifteen-year-old Vitalii pried open the doors of cabinets containing Ukrainian railroad communication and signaling equipment. "You could say I was duped," Vitalii said, describing his situation.

items.title: Mossad chief lauds "groundbreaking" covert operations against Iran, Hizbollah
items.summary: JERUSALEM: The head of Israel's Mossad has praised the spy agency's "groundbreaking" operations in the war against Iran and Hizbollah, saying it acquired intelligence "from the heart of the enemy's secrets." "We acquired strategic and tactical intelligence from the heart of the enemy's secrets," Barnea said, adding that Mossad had "proved new, groundbreaking operational capabilities in target countries." Hizbollah has said it is responding to Israeli ceasefire "violations."

items.title: Perak’s Lenggong earns Malaysia rare dual Unesco status as World Heritage Site and Global Geopark
items.summary: According to Shamsul Anuar, Lenggong Geopark was officially recognised as a Unesco Global Geopark at a ceremony held at Unesco Headquarters in Paris, France, at 3.20pm local time (9.20pm Malaysian time) yesterday. Lenggong was previously gazetted as a National Geopark on December 16, 2021. Shamsul Anuar said Lenggong was recognised as a Unesco Global Geopark for its outstanding geological significance, rich biodiversity and unique cultural heritage.

items.title: Luxury gifts, market manipulation: South Korea's former first lady hit with tougher sentence
items.summary: SEOUL: A South Korean appeals court increased the corruption sentence for former first lady Kim Keon Hee on Tuesday to four years in jail, up from 20 months, after finding her guilty of stock manipulation and bribery. The much heavier penalty came as the court overturned Kim's prior acquittal by a lower court on stock price manipulation charges. "The court sentences the defendant to four years in prison and imposes a 50 million won (US$34,000) fine," the Seoul High Court said in a verdict televised live.

items.title: Cleaner fined RM1,500 over ex-girlfriend's obscene video
items.summary: MUAR: A cleaner was fined RM1,500 by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to possessing and distributing an obscene video of his ex-girlfriend last week. According to the charge, the accused was found in possession of an obscene video of his former girlfriend on his mobile phone. Acting on the report, police arrested the accused and seized his mobile phone for examination.

items.title: Proof of death: Man exhumes sister's remains after bank refusal
items.summary: KEONJHAR: A man exhumed his sister's skeletal remains and staged a protest outside a rural bank in Odisha after failing to withdraw money from her account. The incident occurred about 1pm on Monday at a rural bank in Patana block, Keonjhar. According to the Times of India, reports said Jitu had visited the bank several times but was unable to access the funds due to procedural requirements, including documentation to prove his sister's death.

items.title: Two foreigners arrested for smuggling petrol in modified tanks near border
items.summary: PADANG TERAP: Two foreign women were arrested and their cars seized near the border in Durian Burung yesterday after enforcement officers found additional petrol tanks installed in their vehicles. Kedah Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry chief enforcement officer Zalina Aziz said the suspects, in their 30s and 40s, were intercepted by the General Operations Force (GOF) at 2pm. Authorities seized about 200 litres of petrol worth RM774 and two Proton Wiras.

items.title: Ex-policeman pleads guilty to impersonating officer to steal cigarettes
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A former policeman today pleaded guilty at the Yong Peng magistrate's court to impersonating a civil servant to steal a pack of cigarettes from a convenience store last week. The court, however, postponed sentencing and ordered the accused to undergo psychiatric evaluation at Permai Hospital in Johor Baru on May 25. For the second charge, he was accused of dishonestly stealing a pack of cigarettes under Section 380 of the Penal Code.

items.title: Selangor police chief: 12 officers removed from field duties over suspected crypto robbery probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Twelve police officers suspected of involvement in a cryptocurrency robbery case involving a foreign national are now placed under supervision of their respective unit heads. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said all the officers have been removed from active field duties, according to a report by BuletinTV3. “The investigation is still pending technical reports.

items.title: Iran: US no longer in position to 'dictate' policy to other nations
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran said on Tuesday that the United States was no longer able to "dictate" what other countries do, as Washington weighed a new proposal from Tehran on ending the Middle East war. "The United States is no longer in a position to dictate its policy to independent nations," defence ministry spokesman Reza Talaei-Nik said, according to state TV, adding Washington would "accept that it must abandon its illegal and irrational demands." --AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Part-time driver charged with trafficking over half a tonne of MDMA
items.summary: MELAKA: A part-time driver was charged at the Ayer Keroh magistrate's court here on five charges of trafficking 569.36kg of methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) in March. Chai Koh Chun, 55, nodded in understanding when the charges were read to him in front of magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Suleiman. Under the first charge, the accused allegedly trafficked 269,382.78g of MDMA, and he is accused of trafficking the same drug with a gross weight of 298,650g under the second charge, while the remaining two charges relate to trafficking 176.42g and 150.88g of MDMA and 1,003.30g of ketamine.

items.title: Melaka scam syndicate offered RM2,500 – RM6,000 salaries plus commissions, say police
items.summary: MELAKA: Police have crippled a fraud syndicate involved in love scams, non-existent investments, and sex services scams following raids in Taman Kota Bendahari, Klebang. State police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the simultaneous raids were conducted by the state Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID), Melaka Tengah district CCID, and the state Special Branch at 11am on April 23. They comprise 20 Chinese men, 19 Vietnamese men, four Vietnamese women, one Chinese woman, and one Indonesian woman.

items.title: Caught for trespass, spared prosecution: DPP orders release of ‘joget’ teens at Muhibbah LRT tracks in Bukit OUG to spare their future
items.summary: Cheras District Police said the incident took place at about 11.52pm on April 25 and was detected via closed-circuit television footage showing one of the teenage boys entering the track at Platform 1 to ‘berjoget’. The boys aged 16 and 17 were initially detained by auxiliary police before being handed over to Bukit Jalil police for investigation under Section 62 of the Railways Act 1991 for trespassing on the tracks. They were remanded for two days before being released on police bail on April 26 after the investigation was completed.

items.title: Police working with Interpol to track eHati founder, husband still at large
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 28 — Police are still looking for the founder of a controversial family motivation programme eHati and her husband, who are believed to be abroad, Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said. He added that they are also working with the International Criminal Police Organisation (Interpol) to facilitate efforts to locate the two individuals. However, appropriate actions have been taken by the police to ensure that if they are detected in the relevant countries, arrests can be carried out,” he said at a press conference on the achievements of the Selangor Narcotics and Commercial Crime Investigation Departments here today.

items.title: Two men charged as mule account proxies in online scams
items.summary: Azlan Shah Shahrim Effendy, 30, and Muhammad Dafy Irfan Mohd Asmadi, 19, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read separately before judges Mohd Zamir Suhaimee and Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. The offence was allegedly committed at Horizon Hills, Iskandar Puteri, between May 8 and May 12 last year. The offence was allegedly committed at Jalan Nusa Bestari 9/8, Taman Nusa Bestari, Iskandar Puteri, on Dec 31 last year.

items.title: Court sets Aug 18 trial for Pantai Jerejak assemblyman Fahmi Zainol over alleged wife assault
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, April 28 — The Magistrate’s Court today set August 18 for the trial of Pantai Jerejak assemblyman Fahmi Zainol, charged with intentionally injuring his wife in February. Magistrate Chia Huey Ting set the date following a request by Deputy Public Prosecutor Ikmal Affendi Zulkifly to schedule a trial before getting the expected medical report this week. Fahmi, former Penang Agrotechnology, Food Security and Cooperatives Development Committee chairman, arrived at the court complex with his wife.

items.title: Ramanan: RM9.24mil allocated to 462 temples under Dharma Madani
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of RM9.24 million has been channelled to 462 temples nationwide under the Dharma Madani programme, said Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan. He said the first phase, which began on Jan 29, involved 162 temples with an allocation of nearly RM3.24 million. The second phase begins today, involving RM6 million for 300 temples.

items.title: May 5 EGM to decide future of Saujana Golf and Country Club
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Saujana Resort (M) Bhd will ask shareholders to approve several proposals at an adjourned extraordinary general meeting (EGM) on May 5. The proposals are intended to boost revenue and ensure the long-term sustainability of Saujana Golf and Country Club. In a circular to shareholders, the company said the proposals are intended to enlarge the club's membership, with the objective of improving recurring income.

items.title: Kedah police hunt Sungai Petani assault suspect linked to Johor coma case; one wife miscarried, another gave birth
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, April 28 — Police have confirmed that the suspect believed to have assaulted his wife, causing her to suffer a miscarriage in Ambangan Heights, is the same man involved in a previous case in Johor, in which his wife was beaten into a coma. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the 43-year-old suspect is currently being hunted by police, and the case is being investigated under Section 325 of the Penal Code. “This is the same man involved in the Johor case.

items.title: Teenagers detained for trespassing on LRT tracks to dance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police detained two teenagers after one of them deliberately trespassed onto the tracks at the Muhibbah LRT station and danced on the railway line on Saturday. Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the incident, which occurred at 11.52pm, was detected by security personnel through closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, where one teenager was seen intentionally getting onto the tracks at Platform 1 solely to dance for entertainment purposes. Rosdi said the case is being investigated under Section 62 of the Railways Act 1991 for trespassing on railway tracks.

items.title: Johor tops Malaysia's a leading data centre hub
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor has cemented its position as Malaysia's main data centre growth engine, with JLL Malaysia projecting the southern state to remain the country's dominant digital infrastructure hub as hyperscale operators accelerate expansion. The property consultancy firm said Johor is now the centre of Malaysia's data centre boom, leading the country's rapid capacity expansion on the back of its proximity to Singapore, lower development costs and growing appeal to global technology players. JLL director of capital markets and data centres Keith Eng said Johor now has 850 megawatts of operational data centre capacity, with another 1,800 megawatts under construction and 2,700 megawatts in the pipeline, making it by far the largest and fastest-growing data centre cluster in Malaysia.

items.title: Three teens held over sexual assault against 14-year-old girl in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU: Three teenagers, including two minors, have been arrested for alleged sexual crimes against a 14-year-old schoolgirl in several incidents since late last year. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Jasmin Hussin said the arrests followed a police report lodged last Saturday regarding incidents that allegedly occurred between November last year and February. Following the report, police investigations led to the detention of the three suspects.

items.title: Family of late cook donate his organs in final act of kindness [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The family of the late Muhammad Syukri Bazli hopes his organ donation will offer a second chance at life for those in need. His uncle, Azlan Yahya, 58, said the decision was made to donate his organs on Sunday with the consent of Syukri's parents and his wife, Nurul Hanis Amelia Ramli, 32. He never spoke about organ donation before, but knowing his character, the family felt this was the right decision," he said.

items.title: Muhyiddin trial: Bank flagged inflow of RM19.5m into Bersatu's account, court told [WATCH]
items.summary: In response to deputy public prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin, Norazeanti said the bank issued a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) over 29 transactions linked to the company. Wan Shaharuddin: So, was a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) issued by the bank? Wan Shaharuddin: Why?

items.title: Anwar: Higher education success defined by quality, not just numbers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's standing as a regional education hub must be judged by the calibre of its graduates rather than mere enrolment figures, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. he said in a social media posting Anwar also highlighted the need to balance rapid technological growth with human values. "Values, ethics, and morality must remain our core.

items.title: [UPDATED] RM7.7mil worth of unregistered weight-loss drugs seized at KLIA
items.summary: SEPANG: The Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) has foiled two separate attempts to smuggle unregistered pharmaceutical products through Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA), with total seizures valued at some RM7.7 million. KLIA MCBA commander Mohd Safrie Zakaria said the interceptions, carried out at Terminal 1 and Terminal 2 on consecutive days, underscored the agency's vigilance in safeguarding the country's entry points. He said the operations also reflected close coordination with key agencies, including the Health Ministry, to curb the entry of hazardous and unregistered medical products.

items.title: Selangor sees fall in organ donation rate despite strong awareness
items.summary: Selangor Public Health and Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin said that in line with the decline, the state’s organ donation rate has also fallen from 3.19 per one million population in 2023 to 2.43 per one million last year. “Despite a slight decrease at state level, Selangor’s organ donation rate remains higher than the national average, reflecting strong performance but requiring continued efforts to boost community participation,” she said. Meanwhile, Jamaliah said Selangor is one of the leading states in Malaysia for organ donation pledges, ranking fourth overall with a pledge rate of 1.43 per cent of the population.

items.title: Malaysians among world’s most optimistic about AI, study finds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysia ranks among the top countries in artificial intelligence (AI) readiness, supported by strong adoption rates, public trust and continued investment in the digital economy, according to global real estate property technology group, Juwai IQI Holdings. Kashif said that globally, Malaysians have among the highest levels of optimism towards AI, with about 80 per cent believing the technology will significantly change their lives, while 76 per cent expressed excitement about its potential. “Nearly two-thirds of Malaysians (70 per cent) believe that products and services using AI have more benefits than drawbacks, compared to only 59 per cent of people globally.

items.title: Anwar: Universities must balance knowledge, tech and moral values, not just become cert-producing ‘factories’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — The national education system must be based on a balance between mastery of knowledge, technological advancement and the strengthening of human values, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said educational institutions play an important role in producing a generation that is not only knowledgeable, but also possesses strong morals, ethics and identity. Also present were the Prime Minister’s wife and Bandar Tun Razak MP Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Higher Education Minister Adam Adli Abdul Halim and UCSI University chancellor Tunku Zain Al-‘Abidin Tuanku Muhriz.

items.title: Foreign tech firms must train Malaysians under investment rule, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Foreign technology companies are required to provide high skilled training opportunities to local talent as a prerequisite for investing in Malaysia, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said the government is intensifying efforts to develop AI talent domestically to meet the requirements for the growing high-tech investments in Malaysia. Meanwhile, Anwar said the government’s implementation of a sovereign AI cloud is not intended to restrict investments by global technology companies, but rather involves control over sensitive data within the scope of national security.

items.title: New report confirms Sabah as carbon-negative state, opens door to climate cash
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 28 — Sabah has been officially confirmed as a “carbon-negative” state, meaning it absorbs more carbon than it emits, a finding that could position it to benefit from growing global carbon credit and climate financing markets. Assistant Minister to the Chief Minister Datuk Ceasar Mandela Malakun said the status was established through recent state-led studies under the Sabah Climate Change Action Council – a climate risk vulnerability assessment and the Sabah Greenhouse Gas Inventory Report 2024 – the first subnational assessment of its kind in Malaysia. The studies mark a major milestone in the state’s climate and economic planning.

items.title: No deal after 30 years: Penang CM confirms talks failed before Waterfall Cafe demolition
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 28 — Talks between the Penang government and Waterfall Cafe have failed to produce a settlement, bringing to an end a decades-long dispute that has persisted since 1990, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. “Despite outstanding rental arrears, the state government through the Northeast District and Land Office (PDTTL) offered several reasonable options to Waterfall Cafe to relocate and continue operations,” he said. He said the cafe owners rejected the offers without clear justification.

items.title: As scams hit RM2.9b, Fahmi warns of AI-powered fraud threats, calls for stronger action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysians have lost about RM2.9 billion to online scams, a figure Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today described as a serious and escalating national threat. Speaking at the Google 2026 APAC Online Safety Dialogue, Fahmi said the scale of losses showed the urgent need for stronger coordinated action between the government, tech companies, and regional partners such as Asean. Fahmi praised Google’s efforts in tackling online fraud and said Malaysia is open to deeper collaboration with technology firms and the NSRC.

items.title: Johor Regent meets DPM Fadillah, MB to discuss water supply, energy sustainability
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, April 28 — Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Regent of Johor, received a courtesy call from Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Fadillah Yusof and state leaders today to discuss issues related to water supply and energy sustainability in the state. In a Facebook post today, the Johor Regent said the meeting involved the deputy prime minister, who is also energy transition and water transformation minister, Fadillah; Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi; and Johor State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir. Among the matters discussed were efforts to ensure a more systematic water supply system and sustainable electricity generation to guarantee sufficient resources for Johor’s future development needs.

items.title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Mamfor’s RM19.5m ‘donations’ in 29 cheques to Bersatu flagged as suspicious, unusual to give money to political party, banker says
items.summary: Bank Muamalat’s PKNS Shah Alam assistant branch manager Norazeanti Miswan said this while testifying as the ninth prosecution witness in Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s corruption and money laundering trial. Norazeanti said her bank branch had internally reported each of Mamfor’s 29 Bank Muamalat cheques, due to the “large and frequent withdrawals paid to” Bersatu. Donation, political donation, ‘sumbangan’?

items.title: LHDN ‘killing the golden goose’? Lim Guan Eng questions tax surge, urges aid for struggling MSMEs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Lim Guan Eng has questioned whether Malaysia’s rising tax collection is putting pressure on businesses, warning that overburdening companies could undermine long-term revenue growth. He argued that the jump in tax collection appears disproportionate to Malaysia’s economic growth, noting that gross domestic product (GDP) expanded by just 5.2 per cent in the same year. He warned that without thriving businesses generating income, the government’s ability to sustain tax revenue would be at risk.

items.title: Malaysia submits response to US probe over forced labour, excess capacity claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysia has submitted an explanation to the United States (US) regarding allegations of structural excess capacity and production as well as the use of forced labour, which are among the issues being investigated under Section 301(b) of the US Trade Act of 1974. Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said Malaysia has followed the law accordingly. Recently, the US, through US Trade Representative (USTR) Ambassador Jamieson Greer, launched investigations on 60 economies under Section 301(b) of the US Trade Act 1974, a section created, among other things, to address failures to take action against forced labour.

items.title: Anwar: N. Sembilan assembly dissolution ‘not an issue’ for now amid crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today speculation that the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly would be dissolved “is not an issue yet”. Anwar met the state’s Ruler Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Muniwar last night amid an unfolding political crisis that is threatening to break the Pakatan Harapan-led state administration. Anwar said he supported Tuanku Muhriz’s view.

items.title: ‘Scam Ready Asean’ programme hopes to boost digital safety and scam awareness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — A new regional initiative, “Scam Ready Asean,” was officially launched today at the Google 2026 APAC Online Safety Dialogue event in Kuala Lumpur. The programme is designed to enhance digital safety and combat online scams across Southeast Asia, as cyber threats continue to rise alongside the region’s fast-growing digital economy. Backed by a US$5 million (RM19.7 million) contribution from Google, the initiative will combine scam prevention tools, public awareness efforts, and large-scale digital literacy training across Asean member states.

items.title: Umno denies Perikatan pact to topple N. Sembilan MB Aminuddin, insists no takeover deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Negeri Sembilan Umno has denied any conspiracy or prior arrangement with Perikatan Nasional (PN) to remove Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, insisting that its withdrawal of support was based solely on loss of confidence in the PKR vice-president. Negeri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen were not seeking to take over the state government, but had collectively lost confidence in Aminuddin’s leadership. Jalaluddin said BN had initially been prepared to sit in opposition if PN’s five assemblymen chose to continue backing the Pakatan Harapan-led government, but the situation changed when PN instead indicated willingness to support BN.

items.title: Anwar: Business as usual for N. Sembilan MB Aminuddin amid political turmoil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today he had instructed Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun to carry out his duties as per usual, even as the Negeri Sembilan Umno chapter had openly expressed its intention to form a new government. Umno has 14 of the 36-seated state legislative assembly. Yesterday, Negeri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias declared all 14 Umno assemblymen had withdrawn their support for Aminuddin, citing loss of confidence because of the way the latter handled the royal dispute.

items.title: Zahid backs proposal to upgrade KPDN enforcement division into department
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 28 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has voiced support for the Enforcement Division of the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) to be upgraded to a department to strengthen the division’s capacity in dealing with increasingly complex consumerism and enforcement issues. He said the role of the enforcement team is no longer limited to traditional enforcement, but rather encompasses domestic trade issues, subsidy leakage and cross-border smuggling involving various ministries and agencies. “The time has come for the Enforcement Division to be upgraded to the Enforcement Department.

items.title: Changing MACC chief not enough, wider reforms still needed, says Malaysian Bar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — The Malaysian Bar has welcomed the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as Chief Commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), but said the leadership change alone is not sufficient to strengthen the country’s anti-corruption framework. In a statement today, the Bar said while it acknowledges the end of uncertainty over the MACC leadership transition, questions remain over whether current arrangements are adequate to ensure public confidence and institutional independence. The Bar reiterated its long-standing call for reforms to the MACC, including stronger institutional independence, transparent appointment processes and a fixed tenure for the chief commissioner to avoid recurring uncertainty and annual speculation.

items.title: N. Sembilan crisis to be settled through political negotiations, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — The political crisis in Negeri Sembilan will be resolved through political means, Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. Zahid said the party had never interfered in the administration of the state government or matters involving Negeri Sembilan’s adat laws, according to a report by Sinar Haria n today. BN state chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the decision followed a crisis involving the state’s adat institutions and constitutional matters.

items.title: Sabah to Putrajaya: Pay up the 40pc revenue now, we’ll keep fighting for bigger billions later
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 28 — Sabah has formally asked Putrajaya to immediately pay its 40 per cent revenue entitlement amounting to just over RM3 billion, with state Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun insisting the claim is based on undisputed and publicly available tax data. Masidi said the state government wrote to the federal government on April 10 seeking payment of 40 per cent from two forms of taxes – customs and income tax – collected in Sabah which alone totalled RM7.547 billion in 2025. He said the state respects a Court of Appeal decision on April 6 to defer implementation of Sabah’s 40 per cent entitlement pending an appeal at the High Court in Kota Kinabalu, but emphasised that the decision relates specifically to retrospective claims.

items.title: Will Negeri Sembilan crisis hit PKR-Umno ties elsewhere? Chang Lih Kang says too early to tell
items.summary: IPOH, April 28 — PKR vice-president Datuk Chang Lih Kang today urged caution following recent developments between PKR and Umno in Negeri Sembilan, saying the situation is still evolving and under close observation. This comes after Umno withdrew its support for the PKR-led menteri besar in Negeri Sembilan, raising questions about the stability of cooperation between the two parties elsewhere. Asked about the situation in Perak, Chang said there are currently no signs of similar tensions between PKR and Umno locally.
